{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Experienced Human Resources Manager",
  "description": "A27 Global Services Limited is a professional recruitment and career development company. We specialize in connecting job seekers with the right opportunities while equipping them with the tools to succeed in today&rsquo;s competitive job market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and proactive Human Resources Manager to oversee and manage all HR functions within our organization.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for recruitment, employee relations, performance management, policy implementation, training and development, and ensuring compliance with labor laws and HR best practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with organizational goals.
	Manage the recruitment and selection process.
	Oversee employee onboarding, orientation, and exit procedures.
	Handle employee relations issues and disciplinary matters.
	Develop and implement performance management systems.
	Coordinate staff training and development programs.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, company policies, and HR regulations.
	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Advise management on HR-related matters and workforce planning.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources Management or a related field.
	Must be a certified HR professional with any of the following certifications:
	
		CHRM/CIHRM
		CIPM
		Other recognized HR professional certifications
	
	
	Minimum of 3&ndash;5 years of experience in Human Resources Management.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and leadership skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and HR management systems.
	Gender: Male / Female.


Starting Salary
N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and relevant credentials to: hrconsultant377@gmail.com using subject &ldquo;Application for Human Resources Manager &ndash; Victoria Island&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-23",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "A27 Global Services Limited"
    
  },

Experienced Human Resources Manager Job at A27 Global Services Limited




        
    

        
        A27 Global Services Limited is a professional recruitment and career development company. We specialize in connecting job seekers with the right opportunities while equipping them with the tools to succeed in today’s competitive job market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and proactive Human Resources Manager to oversee and manage all HR functions within our organization.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for recruitment, employee relations, performance management, policy implementation, training and development, and ensuring compliance with labor laws and HR best practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with organizational goals.
	Manage the recruitment and selection process.
	Oversee employee onboarding, orientation, and exit procedures.
	Handle employee relations issues and disciplinary matters.
	Develop and implement performance management systems.
	Coordinate staff training and development programs.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, company policies, and HR regulations.
	Maintain accurate employee records and HR documentation.
	Advise management on HR-related matters and workforce planning.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources Management or a related field.
	Must be a certified HR professional with any of the following certifications:
	
		CHRM/CIHRM
		CIPM
		Other recognized HR professional certifications
	
	
	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in Human Resources Management.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and leadership skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and HR management systems.
	Gender: Male / Female.


Starting Salary
N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and relevant credentials to: [email protected] using subject “Application for Human Resources Manager – Victoria Island” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Manager",
  "description": "Fastizers Food and Confectionery Limited - Our story is one of passion, hard work, and creativity and right partnerships. We can proudly say we are one of Nigeria&rsquo;s fastest growing indigenous FMCG companies.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Agbara, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	To support the creation of a distinctive organisational culture, build a unique employer brand, facilitate its adoption and promotion by employees
	To ensure the organisation has the right talent pool that are effectively deployed, managed, rewarded and engaged in a cost-effective and sustainable manner.


Job Details
Leadership:


	Provide positive and professional leadership to staff in all departments.
	Contribute to the management of Fastizers to support business growth.
	Delegate appropriate responsibility for the provision of HR services whilst retaining overall accountability for the delivery of all services.
	Provide advice and support to all departments and projects within the Group on matters relating to any issue within the remit Human Resources.
	Support the implementation of the Group&rsquo;s HR policy in relation to equality opportunity, Diversity &amp; Inclusion, etc.
	Coach and mentor the Human Resources team to ensure professionalism and the company&rsquo;s core values are maintained.


Core / Technical:


	Oversee the Human Resources function, ensuring the provision of advice on Human Resources matters.
	Monitor and adapt remuneration and entitlement levels to ensure that the pay structure of Fastizers is responsive to the requirements, needs and expectations of staff, as well as market and currency fluctuations.
	Ensure the provision of HR services including recruitment and selection; training and development; performance management; disciplinary and grievance procedures; payroll and pensions.
	Planning, developing and implementing a strategy for Organizational development (covering areas relevant to the organization&#39;s structure).
	Articulates, updates, and executes the Human Resource strategies, policies, and processes in line with the company&rsquo;s business strategy, key business objectives, and pertinent labour laws.
	Develops and communicates approved strategies, policies, and procedures to unit leaders and staff and drives compliance and adoption.
	Reviews the outputs from the periodic performance reviews and moderation exercises. Reports and advises leadership on their implications for operational performance, succession management, and employee engagement.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in any HR related field.
	Minimum of 10 years in Human Resources Management, with at least 6years in a senior HR leadership role.
	Professional HR certification; CIPM, SHRM or CIPD.
	Proficiency in Enterprise HR systems (ERP).
	Proven working experience as HR Manager.
	People oriented and results driven.
	Demonstrable experience with Human Resources metrics.
	Knowledge of HR systems and databases.
	Ability to architect strategy along with leadership skills.
	Excellent active listening, negotiation and presentation skills.
	Competence to build and effectively manage interpersonal relationships at all levels of the company.
	In-depth knowledge of labor law andHR best practices.


Benefits


	Salary: N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.
	Great work culture and multiple opportunities for growth and self-development.
	Annual leave, excluding federal public holidays.
	Health insurance coverage.
	Monthly employer pension contribution.
	Opportunity to shape the future of a dynamic and ever-growing company.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-18",

Human Resources Manager Job at Fastizers Food and Confectionery Limited




        
    

        
        Fastizers Food and Confectionery Limited - Our story is one of passion, hard work, and creativity and right partnerships. We can proudly say we are one of Nigeria’s fastest growing indigenous FMCG companies.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Agbara, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	To support the creation of a distinctive organisational culture, build a unique employer brand, facilitate its adoption and promotion by employees
	To ensure the organisation has the right talent pool that are effectively deployed, managed, rewarded and engaged in a cost-effective and sustainable manner.


Job Details
Leadership:


	Provide positive and professional leadership to staff in all departments.
	Contribute to the management of Fastizers to support business growth.
	Delegate appropriate responsibility for the provision of HR services whilst retaining overall accountability for the delivery of all services.
	Provide advice and support to all departments and projects within the Group on matters relating to any issue within the remit Human Resources.
	Support the implementation of the Group’s HR policy in relation to equality opportunity, Diversity & Inclusion, etc.
	Coach and mentor the Human Resources team to ensure professionalism and the company’s core values are maintained.


Core / Technical:


	Oversee the Human Resources function, ensuring the provision of advice on Human Resources matters.
	Monitor and adapt remuneration and entitlement levels to ensure that the pay structure of Fastizers is responsive to the requirements, needs and expectations of staff, as well as market and currency fluctuations.
	Ensure the provision of HR services including recruitment and selection; training and development; performance management; disciplinary and grievance procedures; payroll and pensions.
	Planning, developing and implementing a strategy for Organizational development (covering areas relevant to the organization's structure).
	Articulates, updates, and executes the Human Resource strategies, policies, and processes in line with the company’s business strategy, key business objectives, and pertinent labour laws.
	Develops and communicates approved strategies, policies, and procedures to unit leaders and staff and drives compliance and adoption.
	Reviews the outputs from the periodic performance reviews and moderation exercises. Reports and advises leadership on their implications for operational performance, succession management, and employee engagement.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in any HR related field.
	Minimum of 10 years in Human Resources Management, with at least 6years in a senior HR leadership role.
	Professional HR certification; CIPM, SHRM or CIPD.
	Proficiency in Enterprise HR systems (ERP).
	Proven working experience as HR Manager.
	People oriented and results driven.
	Demonstrable experience with Human Resources metrics.
	Knowledge of HR systems and databases.
	Ability to architect strategy along with leadership skills.
	Excellent active listening, negotiation and presentation skills.
	Competence to build and effectively manage interpersonal relationships at all levels of the company.
	In-depth knowledge of labor law andHR best practices.


Benefits


	Salary: N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.
	Great work culture and multiple opportunities for growth and self-development.
	Annual leave, excluding federal public holidays.
	Health insurance coverage.
	Monthly employer pension contribution.
	Opportunity to shape the future of a dynamic and ever-growing company.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

In-Building Solutions (IBS) Engineer Job at MERIT Telecoms Limited




        
    

        
        Merit Telecoms (NIG) Limited / Merit Telecoms Nigeria Limited is an independent and recognized leader in providing wireless voice and data turnkey services to the telecommunication industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: In-Building Solutions (IBS) Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Conduct and oversee Technical Site Surveys (TSS) for In-Building Solutions (IBS) projects.
	Prepare comprehensive site survey reports, including RF analysis, coverage assessment, and link budget calculations using appropriate RF planning tools.
	Design Passive, Active, and Hybrid Distributed Antenna Systems (DAS) for commercial, residential and public buildings.
	Develop RF system designs, including antenna placement, cable routing, equipment selection, and coverage optimization.
	Create, modify, and maintain building floor plans, layouts, and RF design drawings using AutoCAD, Visio, or other applicable design software.
	Supervise RF equipment installation, integration, testing, and commissioning activities.
	Perform corrective and preventive maintenance on IBS/DAS infrastructure to ensure optimal network performance and reliability.
	Coordinate contractors, technicians and subcontractors during project execution and maintenance activities.
	Conduct site acceptance testing and quality assurance inspections.
	Analyze and interpret RF test results, including VSWR, DTF  reports.
	Troubleshoot and resolve RF system faults, coverage issues and performance degradations.
	Ensure compliance with customer requirements, industry standards and health and safety regulations.
	Prepare project documentation, technical reports, as-built drawings and maintenance records.
	Collaborate with RF Planning, Transmission, and Project Management teams to ensure successful project delivery 
	Any other official assignment that may be assigned.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
